data.title: Top 3 Stocks Set To Benefit From Musk's DOGE In 2025
data.description:
DOGE Takes On The Department of Education

The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), established by President Trump, is a headline-grabbing initiative aimed at eliminating waste and enhancing operational efficiency in the federal government. Led by tech tycoon Elon Musk, DOGE is leveraging AI to provide a comprehensive review of federal spending to identify areas of waste and abuse. The agency has already made huge cuts to federal spending, with an estimated savings of $105B through a combination of contract cancellations and workforce reductions. These sweeping changes are already creating ripple effects throughout the economy. Federal workforce reductions surfaced in the most recent jobs report, with the U.S. losing 10,000 federal payrolls in February 2025.

DOGE Cuts Just Starting To Impact Federal Payrolls

In addition to slashing the federal payroll, DOGE has specified other areas of reform. On Tuesday, March 11th, the Department of Education announced plans to cut nearly half its staff in anticipation of Trump’s expected executive order to dismantle the agency. Increased scrutiny around procurement spending could have implications for defense contractors. The VA is expecting upwards of 80,000 job cuts in 2025 following the completion of its formal review by DOGE. With entitlement spending under review, healthcare providers could face pressure from potential reimbursement changes. However, pockets of opportunity may exist for sectors tangential to DOGE’s objectives and the broader Trump administration.

1.) Financial Services

The Trump administration has been vocal about the regulatory burden for financial institutions. Acting Consumer Protection Financial Bureau (CPFB) Director Russell Vought has ordered the agency to pause its supervision of companies it oversees. This could benefit big banks, who no longer have to contend with the CFPB’s investigations and enforcement actions.

2.) Technology

Musk has routinely emphasized DOGE’s plans to leverage technologies such as artificial intelligence and blockchain to modernize government systems, promote interoperability, and streamline processes. DOGE is currently deploying AI to automate tasks, analyze contracts, and scrutinize spending. This presents an opportunity for tech companies specializing in digital transformation and process optimization to help in this pursuit

3.) Energy

Fossil fuel companies could benefit from the Trump administration’s potential rollback of climate initiatives, while renewables could face headwinds from reduced government support.

Top 3 Stocks Likely To Be Impacted By DOGE In 2025

As DOGE continues to implement its vision, we feature three U.S. stocks spanning sectors described above with solid investment fundamentals, excellent Quant factor grades, and Quant 'Strong Buy' recommendations.

Additionally, the stocks selected meet the following criteria:

  • 1. Market capitalization > 500M

  • 2. Stock price >$2.00

  • 3. US domiciled companies

1. Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN)

  • Sector: Consumer Discretionary

  • Industry: Broadline Retail

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/11/2025): 5 out of 482

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/11/2025): 6 out of 39

  • Market Capitalization: $2.06T

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

Amazon, a global leader in e-commerce and technology, operates across multiple sectors, including its cloud computing platform Amazon Web Services (AWS). As the dominant cloud computing solutions provider globally, Amazon could benefit from DOGE’s push for cloud adoption across agencies. AWS’ scalability and security features make it a strong candidate for supporting government modernization efforts.

When examining AMZN from a Quant lens, we see it scores highly across most factor grades.

AMZN Factor Grades

The company’s growth is robust in terms of top-and-bottom line and forward and backward looking metrics. AMZN sports a FWD EPS Diluted Growth of 38% which is an incredible 464% higher than the sector median, while its Long-term EPS Growth (3-5YR CAGR) sits 90% above the consumer discretionary sector median growth rate.

AMZN Growth Grade

The company’s ‘A+’ Profitability Grade is supported by an exceptional ROE of 24% coupled with a solid Cash-Per-Share of $7.44 vs. the sector’s 2.59%. While the company’s momentum has faltered in the last three months, this pullback has enabled Amazon to make gains in terms of its valuation. The company’s TTM PEG offers an attractive 43% discount to the sector, and its forward-looking PEG is broadly in line with other Consumer Discretionary stocks. This is a rare opportunity for Amazon, which has been rated a Quant-hold for most of the past year. AMZN’s leadership across cloud computing, AI, and other technological frontiers make it a potential candidate for collaboration with the Trump administration and one stock likely to benefit from Musk’s endorsement of the tech sector.

2. Wells Fargo & Company (WFC)

  • Sector: Financials

  • Industry: Diversified Banks

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/11/2025): 27 out of 669

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/11/2025): 9 out of 66

  • Market Capitalization: $219.59B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

Wells Fargo & Company is a top Quant-ranked Diversified Bank stock and one of America’s largest financial institutions. Recently, the bank has seen a favorable shift in the regulatory environment. The company, which has made huge progress in remediating enforcement actions stemming from 2016 fraud allegations, caught another break last week. The CPFB dismissed a suit against the bank, illustrating the Trump administration’s lighter-touch approach to financial regulation. Wells Fargo is on track for the removal of a $1.95T asset cap restriction that was imposed by the Federal Reserve back in 2018. Lifting the asset cap would give the bank more flexibility to grow its balance sheet and redirect effort toward bank operations.

Robust fee-based revenue helped propel Wells Fargo’s solid growth and profitability in 2024, with net income reaching $5.1B and adjusted EPS climbing 11% Y/Y. The company’s FWD ROE Growth is more than 500% above the sector median, while its TTM One-Year Divided Growth Rate stands at 15.38% vs. the sector’s 4.19%. Analysts are increasingly taking notice of WFC’s financial strength; the company has received an exceptional 15 FY1 up revisions in the last 90 days, versus one downward.

WFC Revisions Grade

WFC’s strong gains over the last year have diminished its overall valuation. However, the stock trades broadly in line with the sector across most of its underlying metrics. When examining its FWD PEG ratio, a combination of a stock’s potential growth relative to its current valuation, we see the stock trading at a 17% discount to the financial sector. WFC is a financial stock with strong fundamentals, and with the help of DOGE, might be able to put its onerous regulatory past behind it, accelerating profitability and future growth.

3. Antero Resources Corporation (AR)

  • Sector: Energy

  • Industry: Oil and Gas Exploration and Production

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/11/2025): 2 out of 231

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/11/2025): 1 out of 71

  • Market Capitalization: $10.79B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

Antero Resources Corporation is an independent oil and gas company focused on exploring, developing, and producing natural gas and oil in the Appalachian region. In its Q4 earnings call, the company highlighted several operational gains that contributed to its strong financial performance for the quarter.

“We reduced the time it takes to drill a well to just 10 days in 2024. This is a nearly 30% improvement, compared to the 14 days that we averaged a couple of years ago, that is 2022,” said Paul Rady, President, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Antero Resources Corporation

The company boasts an overall ‘A+’ Growth Grade, supported by a FWD EBITDA Growth of 28% vs. the energy sector’s 4.34% and an EPS FWD Long-Term Growth (3-5Y CAGR) that is more than 900% above the sector median. These gains have contributed to the stock’s momentum; Antero has strongly outperformed the energy sector on a trailing one-year basis, returning 36.25% vs. -11.40%.

AR Momentum Grade

Antero offers a compelling valuation, with a FWD PEG that is an 87% discount relative to other energy stocks. Wall Street analysts have endorsed AR’s future prospects, with a whopping 11 FY1 up revisions in the last 90 days versus one downward. The company’s excellent investment fundamentals indicate the company is on a solid trajectory, which could be accelerated under the Trump administration and DOGE. Any broader efforts by DOGE to reduce bureaucratic hurdles and expand opportunities for the energy sector could further fuel its growth in 2025.

Concluding Summary

DOGE has already made significant impacts through its expansive review of government spending. The effects of the newly created agency are widespread, with over 10,000 federal job cuts surfacing in the February Jobs Report and the Department of Education slated to reduce its workforce by nearly half. The Department of Veteran’s Affairs is expecting to cut more than 80,000 workers by year-end. Scrutiny over procurement and entitlement reviews could impact the healthcare and defense sectors. Despite these reductions, specific sectors could benefit from the administration’s priorities, including financial institutions facing reduced regulatory oversight, tech companies specializing in digital transformation, and fossil fuel companies that may see a rollback of climate initiatives.

SA Quant has identified potential companies that could take off under DOGE. These companies are rated as 'Strong Buy' by SA Quant, demonstrate strong fundamentals, growth potential, favorable industry, and market positioning.

We have many stocks with strong buy recommendations, and you can filter them using stock screens to suit your specific investment objectives. Consider using Seeking Alpha's 'Ratings Screener' tool to help find stocks that achieve diversification into desired sectors you like. Alternatively, if you're looking for a select number of Quant Strong Buy recommendations on a monthly basis, you might want to explore Alpha Picks.
